request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "C. Hobgoblin Lair" (id: c-hobgoblin-lair), level 1.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- entrance | printed key "ENTRANCE" | Entrance | pages 25
- 16 | printed key "16" | Trapped Hallway | pages 26
- 17 | printed key "17" | Common Room | pages 26
- 18 | printed key "18" | Store Room | pages 26
- 19 | printed key "19" | Sub-Leader | pages 26
- 20 | printed key "20" | Guard Room | pages 26
- 21 | printed key "21" | Chief's Room | pages 26
This level's map is on page 38.

[page 25]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
13. CHIEF'S QUARTERS: The wily orc chief
resides here. A war leader and a couple of
common orcs are also usually found here.
The chief has a key that opens the eastern
temple door at room #9 attached to a necklace
of long pencil-thin gold bars (value 100 gp).
Treasure is housed in a locked chest with a gas
trap inside it. The trap is released when it is
opened, unless some long pencil-sized object
is inserted into a hole in back (the chief's
necklace is used for this). The gas will affect a
10' area around the chest (save vs. Poison or
die; alternately the GM may substitute a
similarly-nasty effect of his or her own
devising). In the chest are 800 gp, 500 ep,
700 sp, and a Potion of Healing.
This room contains various bits of stout
wooden furniture. Under a heavy chair in the
northeast corner is the alarm bell that rings if
either of the trapped secret doors in room #11
is opened without disarming it first.
Orc Chief: AC 15, HD 4, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+2, Mv 20', Sv F4, ML 9, XP 240
HP 18 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Orc War Leader: AC 14, HD 2, #At 1 longsword, 
Dam 1d8+1, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 9, XP 75
HP 10 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
2 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 longsword, Dam 1d8, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8 (9 while chief and/or leader
are present), XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
14. COMMON ROOM: This trash-littered room
houses nine orcs. They are currently throwing
rocks at one who is standing against a wall.
Several others are bruised. The game is to see
who can take the most beating before
flinching. Each has 1d6 sp.
9 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 3 ☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 2 ☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐
15. COMMON ROOM: This room houses seven
orcs. It is cluttered by miscellaneous debris
that they have torn up and discarded, as well
as firewood and stone circles for cooking.
Hidden in a hole in the northeast corner
(covered by a stack of fireplace rocks and a
foot of earth) is a bag with 10 pp and 400 sp.
7 Orcs: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 spear, Dam 1d6, 
Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐ 4 ☐☐☐☐
4 ☐☐☐☐
C. HOBGOBLIN LAIR
These hobgoblins have taken over what was
going to be an expansion of the funeral crypt at
D and are renovating it. The interior is a
mixture of smooth stone where the original
carvers completed their work, rough unfinished
work where they left off, and some work by
the hobgoblins.
ENTRANCE: Twin entrances lead into the first
chamber where five hobgoblins are bunked on
guard duty. Each has 1d6 sp.
At "A" is a wide underground stream being
used as a moat. Wooden planks about 15' long
(used to cross it) are stored on the opposite
side. A loud horn (from an ox) hangs on a peg
and can be blown to attract someone to come
put planks across (the hobgoblins at #17 are
tasked for the job). Other methods can be
used to cross it; if the players engage in a
lengthy debate or over-elaborate plan to cross,
roll 1d6 and on a 5 or 6 two hobgoblins from
#17 appear to interrupt.
In the northwest corner at "C" is a crude hide
painted to resemble stone used for laying
ambushes (i.e. hiding behind the hide and
letting people get inside the room, then rushing
out to attack).
5 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
23

[page 26]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
16. TRAPPED HALLWAY: Three sets of pillars
help support the 20' high ceiling of this
otherwise innocuous hallway. The hobgoblins
have strung thin wires along the floor between
the center two. Anyone tripping the wires will
be attacked by a heavy stone-headed mallet
swinging down from behind the pillars (it
attacks as a 2 HD creature and does 1d8 points
of damage if it hits) followed a moment later
by a net covered in bells that covers the hall
between the two pillars. Anyone caught under
the net will be tangled for 1d6 rounds and their
struggles will cause the bells to ring loudly.
There is a good chance hobgoblins from room
#17 below will hear the bells and come to
investigate.
17. COMMON ROOM: Six hobgoblins bunk here.
Each has 1d6 sp. They are not wearing armor
but will don it if given time. They have fixed
the place up, and are using what was once
going to be platforms for caskets as benches
and tables. Three fancy, elaborate red funeral
shrouds with thin gold threading and black
details are being used as hammocks, each
worth 50 gp.
6 Hobgoblins: AC 14 armored, 11 unarmored, 
HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, Dam 1d8, Mv 30' armored 
or 40', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
18. STORE ROOM: Usually this room is locked
(but not very well – a Thief could pick either
door with ease and the doors can be knocked
in easily). The chief (in #21) has the key to the
doors. It is full of odds and ends and minor
plunder: clothing, sacks of grains and potatoes,
hanging dried meats, perhaps some hanging
"fresh meat" in the form of slain foes. Also
there are mining tools: picks, chisels, hammers,
etc.
19. SUB-LEADER: This room has been taken
over by a skilled and tough hobgoblin, who
lives in luxury. On the wall are a short bow
and two quivers of arrows, as well as other
items. He has two pouches on his belt
containing 300 ep in one and 300 gp in the
other. He carries the key to this room on a
string made of hair on his belt.
Hobgoblin: AC 14, HD 3, #At 1 scimitar or 
1 shortbow, Dam 1d8 scimitar, 1d6 shortbow, 
Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 8, XP 145
HP 15 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
20. GUARD ROOM: The hobgoblins here have
piled up rubble in the hallway and stockpiled
six vials of holy water to fend off the ghouls to
the west. Each has 1d6 sp.
4 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8, XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
21. CHIEF'S ROOM: This chamber contains the
hobgoblin chief and 3 bodyguards. The chief
has the key to the store room at #18. There is
a plain bed, chairs, tables, and other
furnishings here.
A collection of goods on one table includes a
vial with a Potion of Stone to Flesh (1 use in it).
A bag tied to the foot of the bed holds 600 gp,
1,200 sp, and 1,800 cp.
Hobgoblin Chief: AC 15, HD 5, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 20', Sv F5, ML 8, XP 360
HP 25 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐☐☐☐
3 Hobgoblins: AC 14, HD 1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 30', Sv F1, ML 8 (9 while chief is 
present), XP 25 ea.
HP 5 ☐☐☐☐☐ 5 ☐☐☐☐☐
5 ☐☐☐☐☐
D. ABANDONED CRYPTS
These are the old funeral crypts, which were
once going to be expanded at lair C. Once
highly decorated, the facade to the entrance is
now in ruin, with much of the marble overlay
broken off and scattered about, leaving bare
stone. A plaque that once held the name of
the crypts lies in shards on the ground. Bits of
bone, scraps of cloth, and chunks of wood
(some of it varnished) litter the floor and
clutters every floor inside.
In addition to the larger areas, there are five
small, rounded alcoves along the walls (two
east, three west) which contain pedestals for
shrines.
24

areas.description: Twin entrances lead into the first chamber, where five hobgoblins are bunked on guard duty. At "A" is a wide underground stream used as a moat. Wooden planks about 15' long used to cross it are stored on the opposite side, and a loud ox horn hangs on a peg to summon someone to come put the planks across; the hobgoblins in area 17 are tasked with that job. If intruders spend too long debating or over-elaborating a crossing plan, roll 1d6 and on a 5 or 6 two hobgoblins from area 17 appear to interrupt. In the northwest corner at "C" is a crude hide painted to resemble stone, used for ambushes by hiding behind it and letting people enter before rushing out.
features: Wide underground stream at "A" used as a moat
Wooden crossing planks stored on the opposite side
Ox horn on a peg used to summon hobgoblins from area 17 to place planks
Crude stone-painted hide at "C" used as an ambush screen

areas.description: A 20' high hallway supported by three sets of pillars. The hobgoblins have rigged it as a trap corridor.
areas.trap: Thin wires are strung along the floor between the center two sets of pillars. Anyone tripping the wires is attacked by a heavy stone-headed mallet swinging down from behind the pillars; it attacks as a 2 HD creature and deals 1d8 damage on a hit. A moment later, a net covered in bells drops across the hall between the two pillars. Anyone caught under the net is tangled for 1d6 rounds, and their struggles ring the bells loudly, likely alerting hobgoblins from area 17 below.
features: Three sets of supporting pillars
20' high ceiling
Bell-covered net trap that can alert area 17

areas.description: Six hobgoblins bunk here. They are not wearing armor but will don it if given time. The room has been fixed up, using what were once platforms for caskets as benches and tables. These hobgoblins are tasked with bringing planks to cross the entrance moat when summoned by horn.

encounters.count_note: AC 14 armored, 11 unarmored; movement 30' armored or 40' unarmored

treasure: Three elaborate red funeral shrouds with thin gold threading and black details, used as hammocks, worth 50 gp each.
features: Former casket platforms repurposed as benches and tables
Three fancy funeral shrouds used as hammocks

areas.description: Usually this room is locked, though not very well: a thief could pick either door with ease and the doors can be knocked in easily. The chief in area 21 has the key to the doors. It is full of odds and ends and minor plunder, including clothing, sacks of grain and potatoes, hanging dried meats, perhaps some hanging "fresh meat" in the form of slain foes, and mining tools such as picks, chisels, and hammers.
features: Usually locked, with two easily picked or forced doors
Clothing and minor plunder
Sacks of grain and potatoes
Hanging dried meats and possibly slain foes used as "fresh meat"
Mining tools: picks, chisels, hammers, etc.

areas.description: This room has been taken over by a skilled and tough hobgoblin who lives in luxury. On the wall are a short bow and two quivers of arrows, along with other items. He carries the key to this room on a string made of hair on his belt.

encounters.count_note: Sub-leader; HD 3; attacks with scimitar or shortbow

treasure: Two belt pouches containing 300 ep and 300 gp.
features: Luxurious quarters
Short bow on the wall
Two quivers of arrows on the wall
Room key carried on a hair string belt-cord

areas.description: The hobgoblins here have piled up rubble in the hallway and stockpiled holy water to fend off the ghouls to the west.
features: Rubble piled in the hallway as a barricade
Stockpile of six vials of holy water for defense against ghouls to the west

areas.description: This chamber contains the hobgoblin chief and three bodyguards. The chief has the key to the store room at area 18. The room contains a plain bed, chairs, tables, and other furnishings.
treasure: A vial containing a Potion of Stone to Flesh (1 use).
A bag tied to the foot of the bed holding 600 gp, 1,200 sp, and 1,800 cp.
features: Plain bed
Chairs and tables
Other furnishings
Chief carries key to area 18
